summary: Good‑quality soundboard capture of a hot July 4th stadium show, highlighted by an incendiary Watchtower solo and a monster Hell‑in‑a‑Bucket performance.
review: The Miller SBD tape delivers a clean, well‑balanced mix that benefits from a little EQ but sounds solid throughout. Musically the concert shines despite sweltering crowds – Jerry’s guitar (including rare lap‑steel work) and Brent’s keyboards dominate, with All Along the Watchtower and Hell in a Bucket repeatedly called out as standouts. The set flows as a single long marathon geared to Dylan fans, but the Dead keep the energy high with focused first‑set jams and a powerful second half.

ai_rating.rationale: Across >15 reviews the musical performance is praised (average ~4 ★) while the recording is generally regarded as good (~3½ ★). Weighted average yields a solid 4‑star rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clean, relatively crisp mix with a fat low end; some reviewers note audience ambience and recommend EQ boost. No major dropouts, but Dylan’s vocals can be muffled.

show_quality.setlist_flow: One long stadium set built around the Dylan collaboration; first half tight and fast‑paced, second half (with Dylan) retains energy despite heat, delivering several memorable moments.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s lead work is repeatedly praised – soaring solo on Watchtower, rare lap‑steel/ pedal‑steel moments, and a fiery Hell in a Bucket.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent’s keyboards shine (lap‑steel, “Tons of Steel” vibe) and he adds fire to West L.A. Fadeaway.
